Ad Text:
Your chance to build the future of intelligent automation.

Join a team where machine learning isn’t just research—it’s solving real problems, delivering value, and shaping how industries work.

Luke Johnson

AustCorp Executive is partnering with a Perth-based technology manufacturer designing and building specialist robotic and automated electro-mechanical equipment used across for large scale manufacturing.

The role we are recruiting is for a Mechatronics Engineer. This is a hands-on role in a product/OEM setting where you’ll work closely with the mechanical, electrical and software team to deliver robust mechatronic systems, from concept and build through to test, deployment and continuous improvement.

The Role

Design and integrate robotic arm systems, including end-of-arm tooling and cell integration.
Support electrical cabinet / control panel design, reading and producing electrical schematics and documentation.
Develop and maintain PLC software and support overall controls architecture.

Strong hands-on PLC programming experience, plus exposure to HMI/SCADA environments.

Australian working rights required.
What’s in It for you?
This is a company that offers a dynamic and innovative work environment where every team member plays a crucial role in shaping the future of industry technology. You'll be empowered to contribute to meaningful projects, work with industry experts and fast track your career development.

Job Title: Lead AI Engineer – Global Private Investment & Trading Ecosystem
Ad Text:
Lead AI Engineer – Global Private Investment & Trading Ecosystem

Sydney CBD based

Build a high-performance investment & trading system using AI-powered development tools

Chinese-speaking will be advantaged but is not necessary

Role Overview

In 2026, the divide between private and public markets is dissolving through intelligence. Our client is seeking a Lead AI Engineer to spearhead the continuous evolution of our global investment ecosystem. This platform already centralises access to private companies (including unicorns) and a comprehensive suite of funds - spanning Debt, Equity, Real Estate, and VC/PE - all supported by a robust AI-Blockchain-Wallet stack.

Your mission is to upgrade this "financial nervous system" into an interconnected powerhouse. You will lead the transition from generative tools to Agentic AI, while architecting high-performance API integrations that bridge our ecosystem with external digital platforms to attract a new wave of global investors.

Key Responsibilities

Platform Upgrades & Agentic Shift: Drive the platform's evolution by implementing Autonomous AI Agents capable of end-to-end investment workflows - from multi-fund due diligence to automated on-chain rebalancing.

Ecosystem Interoperability (API First): Architect and govern secure, agent-consumable APIs using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to allow external platforms to seamlessly "plug in" to our investment products.

Intelligent Investor Acquisition: Develop predictive models that analyze external platform data via APIs to identify and attract retail, HNW and institutional investors through hyper-personalised offerings.

Blockchain & Wallet Orchestration: Enhance our Virtual Wallet infrastructure with Account Abstraction and AI-driven security to automate cross-platform capital calls and distributions.

Continuous MLOps Excellence: Lead the "Proof-to-Productivity" phase by ensuring all AI deployments deliver measurable ROI, leveraging advanced observability to monitor agent-driven API traffic and performance.

Regulatory & Trust Guardrails: Implement Explainable AI (XAI) layers to maintain institutional trust and comply with 2026 global standards.

Required Technical Stack

AI/ML Core: Mastery of Python, PyTorch, and agentic orchestration frameworks like LangGraph or CrewAI.

API & Integration: Expert-level REST/GraphQL API design and experience with AI-aware gateways (e.g., Kong, Apigee) for managing agentic traffic.

Web3 Infrastructure: Proficiency in Solidity, RWA Tokenisation (ERC-1400/3643), and MPC (Multi-Party Computation) for secure wallet management.

Data & Storage: Experience with Vector Databases (Pinecone, Weaviate) and scalable Azure/AWS AI infrastructures.

Qualifications

Experience: 4+ years in software engineering.

Please note applications will only be considered from candidates who are currently Australian citizens or permanent residents.

Please forward your CV to applications@financerecruitment.com.au

Please note that all applications are strictly confidential and only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

Senior Data Engineer

Sydney – Hybrid | $150 – $160k + Super | 2 Year Fixed-Term

We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Engineer to join one of Sydney’s fastest growing local councils and play a key role in transforming their enterprise data platform.

This is an exciting opportunity to work with modern Azure cloud data technologies and help build scalable data solutions that support data-driven decision making across the organisation.

The Role

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will design and develop enterprise data pipelines, contribute to the organisation’s data warehouse capability and help implement best practice data engineering and governance frameworks.

You will collaborate with technical and business stakeholders to deliver reliable, scalable and analytics-ready data solutions.

Key Responsibilities
Design and develop enterprise data warehouse solutions
Build and maintain scalable data pipelines
Develop ETL frameworks using Azure Data Factory and Databricks
Design and implement robust data models
Optimise SQL and data processing workflows
Support data governance, documentation and delivery standards
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver data and analytics solutions
Skills & Experience

Essential

5+ years experience in Data Engineering
Strong experience with Azure data platforms
Expertise in SQL and Python
Experience with ETL pipelines and data modelling
Experience building scalable cloud data architectures
Experience with CI/CD or DevOps practices
Desirable

Power BI reporting experience
Experience working in Local Government
Exposure to Azure Purview or modern data governance frameworks
Experience working with Agile tools such as Jira or Confluence
What’s On Offer
Salary: $150,000 – $160,000 + Super
Hybrid working environment
2 year fixed-term contract
Opportunity to work with modern Azure data technologies
Join a rapidly growing organisation investing heavily in data
Referral Bonus

Know someone perfect for this role?

We offer a generous referral bonus for successful placements, so feel free to share this opportunity with your network.

For a confidential discussion about this role, please reach out to Lee Bartlett - lee@bluefinresources.com.au

Overview

Office Data Loader team is hiring Software Engineers to build one of the most largest hyper-scale data ingestion service in the world for Office365 services. 

With hundreds of millions of users using Office365 services, huge amount of data are generated every day. Data is mission critical for Office365 services. It helps engineering teams to understand the quality of live services, generate insights of feature usage and user behavior, troubleshoot bugs and issues. It also offers better visibility to product and management teams on business impacts, empowers product and scientist teams to build intelligence features. 

To support the continuously growth of business needs from Office365, Office Data Loader team builds the hyper-scale distributed data ingestion service. We directly manages the ingestion service on more than 300,000 Exchange Online machines, which upload more than 30,000 TB data to our hyper-scale distributed storage every day. We also support the ingestion service running on even more machines for other Office365 products. 

As the fundamental data ingestion service, we play an incredibly important role to make sure data uploading meet the privacy and compliance requirements. Sensitive data needs to be transformed to prevent privacy leaks or ne. Moreover, privacy leak detection and resolution are also built by us as the safeguard. User data that empowers Machine Learning scenarios has even higher requirements on the design of our service. Privacy and compliance are always our top priority, because Microsoft runs on Trust! 

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities

The quality of the service means a lot for the success of Office365 services. You will have plenty of opportunities to take on challenges to achieve high uploading completeness (>99.9%) and make sure privacy and compliance requirements are always met.   

Design and build high quality, performant and reliable distributed ingestion service.

Design and build modernized telemetry/monitoring/reporting systems to get the insights of service quality.

Improve service performance and efficiency to reduce COST of service.

Work with downstream data consumer teams in different geo locations to onboard data ingestion service, and provide supports in daily work.

Work with privacy & compliance teams to design and build solutions to prevent/detect/resolve privacy issues, also provide guidance and best practice for engineering teams within Office365.

Responsible for the daily operations and maintenance of the live service and ensure quality of service.

Collaborate with other Office365 teams to manage their data ingestion service, provide solutions and guidance. 

Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related technical field AND 2+ years technical engineering experience with coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, or Python

OR equivalent experience.

Other Requirements: 

Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:

Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.

Preferred qualifications:

Master's Degree in Computer Science or related technical field AND 3+ years technical engineering experience with coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, or Python

OR Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related technical field AND 5+ years technical engineering experience with coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, or Python 

OR equivalent experience.

3+ years of experience in software product development.

3+ years’ experience working with distributed system design and debugging.

Passionate to solve hard problems.

Passionate to learn new skills/knowledge.

Knowledge and experience of distributed system, large-scale big data platform technologies is a plus.

Knowledge and experience of performance tuning is a plus.

Knowledge and experience of data privacy is a plus.

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

Purpose of the Role

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to bridge the gap between frontline field operations and cutting-edge digital transformation. In this role, you will document end-to-end job pack processes, uncover operational pain points and undocumented workarounds, and translate those processes into structured inputs suitable for AI-enabled automation and optimization.

Key Responsibilities

Field-Based Discovery: Understand and document field-based and operational workflows by engaging frontline staff and SMEs through interviews and observations.

Process Mapping: Produce high-quality process maps, swimlanes, system interaction diagrams, and data mappings.

AI Strategy: Identify specific areas where AI or automation can deliver measurable operational value

Technical Collaboration: Work closely with technical teams to ensure business intent is preserved during the transition to automated solutions.

Business Case Support: Develop problem statements, impact assessments, and inputs for business cases.

Essential skills and experience

BA Fundamentals: Strong background in problem definition and root cause analysis.

Operational Expertise: Proven experience documenting operational or field-based processes, specifically within utilities or asset-heavy regulated environments.

Stakeholder Engagement: Exceptional ability to work effectively with non-technical frontline staff to gain trust and buy-in.

Data Literacy: Deep understanding of process-to-data relationships to ensure workflows are ready for digital translation.

Experience translating manual processes into AI-enabled solutions or automation.

Understanding of AI applications regarding workflow optimization and decision support.

Previous experience working on early-stage or "first-of-type" initiatives.

What success looks like:

Success in this role means accurately capturing processes that are trusted by field teams while ensuring data for AI use cases is clearly defined and fit for purpose. Ultimately, your work will ensure AI opportunities deliver practical value and that all stakeholders remain aligned with the digital roadmap.

For more information, please reach out to Aimee Senior at Morgan McKinley

About the job Data Engineer/ETL Developer

We are Fujitsu

We use technology to make happier lives. We are a global leader in technology and business solutions that transform organisations and the world around us. We have a long heritage of bringing innovation and expertise, continuously working to contribute to the growth of society and our customers.

About the role

RFQ Opportunity

2-3 months Contract

Melbourne based

Onsite

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
12-month fixed term contract
Contribute to the delivery of an enterprise technology transformation program
Flexible work from home arrangement (3 day's in the office)

We are seeking a Data Engineer to contribute towards the development of Microsoft Azure Data Platform and develop business intelligence solutions. The role will be focused on building out a secure, stable, reliable enterprise data platform.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and maintain optimal data pipeline architecture and implementing appropriate data access controls.
Identify, design, and implement internal process improvements: automating manual processes, optimising data delivery, reliability, efficiency and quality
Identity, evaluate and prioritise technical improvements to the Data Platform and implement them with the support of the Data Team Lead.
Build pipelines and procedures to transform raw data sets into the Enterprise Data Model and analytical models
Collaborate with stakeholders including internal and external teams to assist with data-related technical issues and support their data needs
Maintain the Enterprise Data Model and design appropriate analytical data models according to requirements

Required Skills & Experience:

Broad working knowledge of enterprise data platforms, including operational integration, data modelling, data governance, information management, provisioning, with exposure to BI and analytics
Strong technical skills in data integration, transformation and modelling
Experience in relevant Microsoft Azure data products (e.g. ADF, Synapse, Data Lake, SQL, etc)
An understanding of Delta Lake Framework is desired.
Strong communication skills, analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to initiate practical solutions, using a mix of technologies

Please apply for a confidential chat.

Shape Your World

At Alcoa, you will become an essential part of our purpose: to turn raw potential into real progress. The way we see it, every Alcoan is a work-shaper, team-shaper, idea-shaper & world-shaper.

What’s on offer

A permanent job with long-term career development in our local or global operations.

Annual increases built into your compensation package with many benefits unique to Alcoa.

More time doing the things you love with a leisure day scheme offering 13 additional days leave

Working locally, in an onsite environment enabling you to return home to friends or family every night.

Inclusion and diversity networks; shaping a culture where everyone is welcome, respected and heard.

Be part of our commitment to sustainability, delivering world class technology and innovations that lead the aluminium industry!

A workplace culture that strongly values your safety.

This is your opportunity to shape your career through the skills and experience you bring to the role now and by training for the roles you may want in the future. Our success depends on great teams, where you can thrive and do your best work in a supportive environment.

About the Role

This is a permanent opportunity, starting with a three-year customised graduate development program. You will rotate across various specialised departments at our aluminium smelter in Portland, Victoria. Stay connected with your community, hobbies, friends & family! Our sites are all within driving distance; achieve the ultimate balance in life without having to miss out on hands-on site experiences.

What you’ll be doing

As a Graduate Information Systems Consultant at Alcoa, you to learn, grow, and develop hands-on experience and expertise in:

Identifying and resolving technical problems related to manufacturing and process computer applications, focusing on automation and business equipment.

Assessing and interpretating user Manufacturing and Process control and automation requirements, and the subsequent design and implementation of solutions to satisfy these requirements, to project management and ITAS standards; and

Identifying emerging technologies both within and outside Alcoa with the potential of reducing the complexity and cost of processing operations.

Throughout the program, you will develop to become a driving force behind supporting and advancing computer systems, ensuring compliance, and spearheading innovation within our dedicated smelting business area.

Development and mentoring

Throughout the program, you will be provided with:

coaching, mentoring, hands-on and professional training.

diverse experiences; working all across the smelter.

an increasing level of responsibility

an opportunity to use your innovative thinking, your motivation to learn and passion for growth to shape the future of our business.

About you

To play a part in our ongoing success we are seeking someone who:

Drives a safe, inclusive and collaborative environment by actively listening, valuing diverse perspectives and working constructively with others.

Communicates clearly and effectively, tailoring communication to the audience and ensuring messages are understood through open, two‑way dialogue.

Prioritizes, is decisive and executes by analysing information, identifying solutions and focusing on what moves the business forward.

Takes accountability through owning commitments, showing initiative, and following through to deliver high‑quality outcomes.

Continuously learns, adapts and grows by embracing new challenges, seeking feedback, and using creativity and collaboration to solve problems and improve processes.

Become a valued part of the team that’s reinventing the aluminium industry for a sustainable future, revolutionising the way the world lives, builds, moves and flies. 

If you are already graduated or on the path to graduate soon with tertiary qualifications in Information Technology or Engineering or a related discipline, we want to hear from you!

Recruitment Process

Submit online application (resume, cover letter, transcript).

Complete 10-min video interview.

Finish psychometric testing (~1hr).

Attend Assessment Centre or Panel interview.

Reference check.

Pre-employment medical.

Be part of shaping our organisation and join us in building a legacy of excellence for future generations.

#AlcoaAUS #LI-LW1

About the Location

Our Portland Smelter is a joint venture between Alcoa of Australia (55%), CITIC (22.5%) and Marubeni Aluminium Australia (22.5%). Alcoa manages the day-to-day operations at the smelter and we produce approximately 19 per cent of Australia’s total aluminium production in Portland.  We are proud of the long-standing relationships we have within our local community and are committed to supporting social and diverse causes.

We are values led, vision driven and united by our purpose of transforming raw potential into real progress.  Our commitments to Inclusion, Diversity & Equity include providing trusting workplaces that are safe, respectful and inclusive of all individuals, free from discrimination, bullying and harassment and that our workplaces reflect the diversity of the communities in which we operate.    

This is a place where you are empowered to do your best work, be your authentic self, and feel a true sense of belonging. Come join us and shape your career!  

Your work. Your world. Shape them for the better.

Brunel is representing a leading organisation in the defence maritime sector undertaking major programs across shipbuilding, sustainment and advanced manufacturing. This role offers the opportunity to contribute to nationally significant projects while working alongside highly skilled project, production and commercial teams.

Client Details
Our client is a major Australian defence maritime organisation delivering complex manufacturing and sustainment programs that directly support national capability. Operating in one of the country's most advanced industrial precincts, they are committed to innovation, safety and excellence in delivery. Their projects span multi-year defence programs, offering stability, career growth and exposure to world-class engineering environments.

Description
We are seeking an experienced Configuration Engineer to support the delivery of configuration management across a complex defence shipbuilding program.

This role will work closely with engineering, production, quality and integrated logistics teams to manage configuration data, support change control processes and maintain accurate baselines across digital systems. You will play a central role in ensuring data integrity, system integration and the technical accuracy of configuration reporting throughout the product lifecycle.

In this role you will:

Develop, maintain and monitor configuration data across design and production environments.
Support the implementation of configuration management processes aligned to defence standards.
Administer and improve PLM and configuration tools including Teamcenter, TCRA, Jira and Power BI.
Prepare Configuration Status Accounting and baseline reports using data analysis and scripting methods.
Carry out data analysis to support audits, change impact assessments and traceability checks.
Support engineering change workflows and ensure accurate transfer of data across systems.
Develop automated reporting and dashboards that provide real-time visibility of configuration status.
Resolve technical issues linked to system integrations, data accuracy and reporting.
Profile
You will bring proven experience in configuration management or data-focused engineering roles within heavy industry, defence or complex digital environments.

To be successful, you will have:

Degree in Engineering, Information Systems, or a related technical discipline.
Experience working with PLM systems, preferably Siemens Teamcenter, in an engineering or manufacturing setting.
Capability in SQL, Python or similar methods for data extraction and analysis.
Strong analytical skills supported by attention to detail and a focus on data integrity.
Experience interpreting technical, regulatory and operational information and applying it within structured processes.
Effective communication skills suited to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Understanding of IT environments, system landscapes and basic troubleshooting in support of integrated digital tools.
Experience with Power BI or similar data visualisation tools is valued.
Job Offer
This is a rare chance to work inside Australia's defence maritime sector on projects that directly support national capability. You'll join an organisation committed to excellence, continuous improvement and long-term career development.

Benefits of this role include:

Working on nationally significant defence maritime programs.
Long-term career pathways within a major industrial precinct.
A collaborative, safety-first environment.
Competitive salary and ongoing professional development.

About Us

SMC Corporation is a manufacturer of electro - pneumatic and industrial automation components, successful in providing innovative products and solutions to customers across a wide array of industries. Our success has been achieved through our commitment to providing customers with Japanese quality, customer centric design, local manufacturing and unmatched commercial support.

About the Position:

We are looking for a talented Technical Design Engineer to create innovative, compliant, and high-quality engineering solutions that comply with statutory regulations, meet technical requirements, and uphold SMC quality standards. This role supports engineering operations while providing technical expertise and customer support to internal and external stakeholders. You will report to the Controls Engineering Team Leader and be an integral part of our national control systems engineering team.

About the role

You’ll be responsible for

Design and develop products and systems that meet Australian regulatory requirements and SMC technical, quality, and cost standards.

Collaborate with Engineering and cross-functional teams to ensure designs are fit-for-purpose, cost-effective, and aligned with project scope.

Produce technical documentation, including design specifications, manuals, and commissioning reports.

Maintain up-to-date technical knowledge of products and support quality assurance as required.

Assisting Sales with accurate costing, design info, and solutions for customer enquiries

Managing customer relationships throughout the enquiry process

Supporting product design, development, and manufacturing changes

Conducting site visits and liaising with customers on technical requirements

Providing prompt technical support to Production and other stakeholders

Ensure compliance with Australian and international design standards.

Verify quality throughout the design and manufacturing process, including inspections, testing, and root cause investigations.

Manage design changes, RFS/RDC requests, and technical documentation in line with QMS processes.

Prepare and maintain technical and operational references for production and maintenance.

What We Offer:

Join a globally respected brand and market leader with highly skilled technical and people managers across ANZ

Collaborative environment providing resources and support to be successful.

Work from our Hills Shire office with onsite covered parking, located at a large company-owned site and just minutes from public transport and the train station.

Internal progression opportunities

Insurance benefits within the company superannuation plan

Focus on employee Wellbeing & Employee Assistance Programs

Competitive salary package (with annual Salary Review process).

Qualifications & Skills: 

Minimum 3 years’ relevant engineering experience in a manufacturing/industrial environment (essential).

Tertiary qualification in Engineering is essential: Bachelor of Electrical Engineering (preferred) or Bachelor of Mechanical/Mechatronics Engineering with relevant industry experience.

Electrical Licence is an advantage.

PLC programming experience (highly desirable), including Omron, Allen-Bradley (Rockwell Automation), or equivalent.

Proficient in 2D/3D CAD software (SolidWorks preferred).

Strong understanding of Australian and international design, quality, and compliance standards

Skilled in costings, scheduling, and scope estimation to support customer and sales enquiries

Proficient in root-cause analysis, problem-solving, and data-driven decision making

Commercially aware with the ability to evaluate risks, change control, and lifecycle impacts

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

Detail-oriented with strong organisational skills and the ability to prioritise competing tasks

Familiarity with Quality Management Systems (QMS) and technical documentation processes

 

OUR RECRUITMENT PROCESS

At SMC, we are committed to fostering diversity, equity and inclusion. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply and are dedicated to ensuring a fair and equitable recruitment process.

If your experience matches the role's requirements, click 'Apply' and we'll reach out within a few business days to arrange an initial phone screening

An innovative AI focused technology team is building the next generation of enterprise AI agents and intelligent automation systems for global organisations. They are looking for a Senior AI Engineer (Software) to help design and deploy production grade AI solutions that automate complex workflows and integrate with enterprise systems. This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, generative AI, and enterprise platforms, with a strong focus on building scalable AI applications powered by large language models and agent based architectures. You will be working with modern AI frameworks and cloud technologies to develop AI powered assistants, agent orchestration systems, and enterprise automation platforms that deliver real business value.

The Role
As a Senior AI Engineer you will be responsible for designing and building AI-powered systems that replicate and automate complex human workflows inside enterprise environments. You will collaborate with engineers, product teams, and enterprise stakeholders to translate business challenges into scalable AI solutions.

Key Responsibilities
Design and develop AI agents and AI powered assistants to automate enterprise workflows
Build scalable backend systems using Node.js and TypeScript
Develop LLM powered applications using frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph and Flowise
Implement retrieval augmented generation (RAG) architectures and vector database integrations
Integrate AI solutions with enterprise APIs, internal platforms, and data systems
Design and deploy cloud native applications across AWS, Azure or GCP
Containerise and deploy applications using Docker and Kubernetes
Build and orchestrate multi agent AI workflows for complex enterprise use cases
Work closely with stakeholders to deliver secure, scalable AI solutions
Requirements
Strong software engineering background with 5+ years experience building production systems
Advanced experience with Node.js and TypeScript

Who are we?

So you might ask, who's CreditorWatch? Well, we are a leading Australian data and technology company that provides businesses with access to unique data and innovative products. By using our platform, our customers can confidently manage their commercial relationships, improve productivity and reduce financial risk.

As a commercial credit reporting bureau, we offer a complete suite of credit reporting products and data insights covering the entire customer lifecycle, from customer onboarding and credit decision automation to credit risk management and automated collections.

We were established in 2010 and most recently were named as one of AFR's Top 10 Best Places to Work as well as certified by Great Place to Work consecutively across 2022-2025.

We saw significant growth in 2025 and that's not about to change. We are on track to break records in 2026, scaling at pace, making this the perfect time to join CreditorWatch.

Our Purpose

✅ Empower Australian businesses to trade confidently with their customers.

Our Mission

🏆 We aim to be number one in our industry by delivering unique data insights and innovative products.

Your Role & Team

In this newly created role, we are looking for an experienced ML & AI Engineer to help us accelerate the deployment and operationalisation of predictive models at CreditorWatch.

As part of a ML and AI team within a rapidly growing Data function, you’ll be building scalable ML and AI infrastructure, managing model and feature artefacts, and ensuring robust production workflows.

You will work closely with data scientists and engineering teams to bring models from development to production, with a strong emphasis on reliability, governance, and performance. Our infrastructure is built on AWS, with a data and analytics platform on Databricks.

You'll report directly to the Machine Learning Engineering Manager. Please note, it's a full-time opportunity offering hybrid working conditions out of our Sydney CBD Office.

We are also open to the successful candidate being Melbourne-based.

Some of your responsibilities include and are not limited to:

Build and design production-grade ML systems and lifecycle management.
Develop and maintain feature stores, model registries, and artefact tracking systems to support reproducibility and governance.
Collaborate with data scientists to transition models from research to production, ensuring scalability and compliance.
Build and maintain MCP servers, vector databases to design, support, and implement AI initiatives.
Our ideal candidate
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in machine learning engineering, with a strong focus on production deployment and MLOps.
Proven experience with ML platform tools such as MLflow, Sagemaker, Vertex AI, or similar, and cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and integrated data platforms, Databricks, Snowflake).
Deep understanding of AI/MLOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, monitoring, and end-to-end model lifecycle management in production environments.
Experience building MLOps in Databricks highly regarded.
Strong experience in Python, SQL and/or other codebases.

About the Role

The Machine Learning Engineering Manager is the foundational engineering lead within icare’s Data Science and AI function. The role begins as a hands-on technical lead responsible for establishing robust ML engineering, MLOps and LLMOps practices that enable secure, reproducible, and reliable deployment of AI systems

icare day – access to an extra day’s leave

Access to our Employee Assistance Program

Duties

It’s an exciting time at icare where no two days are the same. In this role you will

Deliver data and Machine Learning and AI models that align to the overall technical, data and AI strategy, making trade-offs where appropriate
Deliver coherent and technically feasible ML, LLM and AI system designs with multiple components interacting across API or system boundaries
Design ML, LLM and AI system metrics and an implementation and monitoring plan to ensure ML, LLM and AI systems are performant through the lifetime of the system
Identify and resolve technology limitations, designing sustainable long-term solutions that minimise risk and maximise value
Drive innovation by formulating complex business objectives into intelligent and scalable ML, LLM or AI solutions
Drive the creation and maintenance of standards to ensure quality delivery of the ML, LLM and AI solutions across the organisation
Formulate plans to improve ML Engineer and Data Scientist efficiency as measured by cycle time, or other similar measurements
Provide constructive feedback and hands-on technical guidance to members of the Data Science and Machine Learning engineering teams through the lifecycle of an ML, LLM or AI project
Partner with the Head of Data Science and AI and the Data Science Manager to set the cultural tone for the team, supporting an environment of psychological safety, empathy and productivity
Help the ML Engineering team build relationships across icare, creating connection across teams
Structure the ML Engineering team so the right people are in the right roles with clearly defined responsibilities
Foster the career growth of ML Engineers and Data Scientists through coaching, mentoring and on-the-job-experiences
Interview and assess candidates to help us build a diverse and talented ML Engineering team 
Partner with Data Platforms and Insights, Architecture, Security, Privacy, and Governance to ensure ML, MLOps, LLMOps and practices align with enterprise standards
Build deep cross-functional relationships, facilitate the right conversations, balancing multiple perspectives, and engage in productive conflict with thoughtful questioning/challenging, disagreeing and committing when necessary to move decisions forward
Advise on the technical ML, MLOps. LLMOps components of an enterprise level AI strategy
Resolve cross-team dependencies to ensure the successful execution of ML, LLM, and AI projects

Skills & Experience

Minimum 8 years’ experience in Machine Learning Engineering, Machine Learning Operations, or DevOps roles
Experience leading or mentoring teams.
Experience deploying ML systems into production environments (cloud or hybrid).
Experience collaborating with Data Scientists, Architects, Security, Privacy, Governance and Platform teams.
Experience with AWS and/or Snowflake based ecosystems.
Experience working in an Agile Scrum environments and in the use of work tracking tools such JIRA.
